rulings.Case Title: CONS. OF PATRICIA ANN FIEBIG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #8, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/9/25. Examiner's Notes sent with the following issues: 1) Ex Parte Petition to Increase Bond has not been filed. 2) Notice of Hearing has not been filed. 3) The beginning of account balance must be the same as the end of account balance of the previous accounting ($124,571.60; the beginning of account balance is currently listed as $124,511.70). 4) The POH Change in Assets listed in the Summary of Account reflect differences in fair market values at the beginning and end of accounting period; however, accountings must be based on carry values. Changes to fair market values cannot be a part of the accounting (fair market value schedules are informational only). a. The $13,226.40 increase in savings bond should only be reflected in the information only fair market value schedule. Only the carry value ($66,104.00) should be used in the actual accounting. b. It is unclear why there are differences in fair market values of cash assets. Only carry values should be used, and any differences in values should be explained in the receipts and distributions schedules. 5) Please allege whether there was an order authorizing payment to James Shields on 5/31/22 for $3,000. If not, please explain why those attorneys fees were paid without a prior court order. 6) Savings bonds and IRA accounts (which appears to actually be an annuity and should be labelled as such) are not cash assets. Please submit a revised End of Account schedule. The individual bonds must also be listed by their serial numbers. 7) Property on Hand schedule lists $32,285.61 in Union Bank acct ending 2164; however, the bank letter attached is from US Bank and the savings account number ends in 0281. 8) Summary of Account on the mandatory Judicial Council Form has not been filed. 9) Proposed order has not been filed.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JAMES FAMILY TRUST DATED SEPTEMBER 13, 1973
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/7/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Trust petitions require 30 days' notice. (2) A copy of the Letters issued to Phoebe Presber James, certified within 60 days, showing that she is the executor of her father's will, does not appear to have been filed yet. (3) How old are the minor beneficiaries of the GST trusts whose interests could be affected? Who represents their interests? See CRC 7.51(d) re notice to minors. (4) Not all beneficiaries of the GST trusts (mentioned in footnote 2 on page 5 of the petition) appear to have been given notice. (How is a "custodian" living in another country the person to receive notice on behalf of a minor?) Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 4/23/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STELLA STAVROS KINGAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/14/25 for an appearance. (No earlier availability.) (1) The accounting was extended to 12/18/24, but the administrator proposes to distribute assets purported to be on hand as of 2/18/25. (At least the proposed distribution amount is greater than the ending balance shown in the accounting.) (2) Paragraph 30 of the third amended petition appears to have an incorrect calculation of the administrator's compensation. Does the administrator wish to waive any higher compensation she might have requested? (3) The proposed distribution to Robert Kingas's heirs is less than the amount set forth in Probate Code Section 13100. If his three children are his successors-in-interest, and no estate has been opened for his estate or he did not have a trust, they should be able to claim their interest via a 13100 affidavit. See LRSF 14.35 G. (4) The airfare for the administrator's cousin and father totaled less than $1,000, but there were also meals and hotel costs, etc., totaling over $13,000. How did she determine that she "professionals... would likely have charged more" than she paid for her family? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 4/1/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BARBARA MOLLER LYONS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/7/25. Examiner's Notes sent to attorney with the following issues: 1) Extraordinary fees are requested by the Petitioner on sale of real property in the amount of $97,775.30. a. The timesheets appear to only show that $19,025 in services were provided. b. As part of its decision regarding the reasonableness of extraordinary fees, this court generally requires a showing that statutory fees are not sufficient to compensate for all services performed [Estate of Walker 221 Cal.App. 2d 792]. Please provide a verified statement of ordinary services provided and timesheets. c. The executor is requesting $475 per hour. Please allege why attorney rates are being charged for services as personal representative. If legal services were provided, they should be separated from non-legal services. Timesheets for ordinary services provided by the attorney for executor must also be provided to determine whether the services would have been covered by statutory fees by attorney. 2) Schedule of assets on hand at the end of the account must be provided. 3) Ending balance of cash (showing the end of account date) must be verified [Local Rule 14.34D]. Please also make sure to file a verified declaration pursuant to Local Rule 14.59A certifying that the original document(s) will be available for inspection in the same way as documents are maintained under CRC 2.257. 4) Petitioner's creditor's claim filed on 1/10/24 in the amount of $4,987.50 appears to have been paid without prior court approval. 5) Probate Code 9202(b) has been revised to require notice even if heirs/beneficiaries have been previously confined. Please provide revised allegation re: California Victim Compensation Board. 6) The Notice of Hearing filed on 12/19/24 appears to be missing pages to the Proof of Service (which contains an incomplete list). The notice also states as what was filed as being a "Petition to File Final Distribution", but it is a "Petition for Final Distribution" The notice must be sent again. 7) Supplement filed on 2/25/25 amends the distribution amounts for Robert Swayne Lyons and Richard Guise Lyons and thus an "Amendment to the Petition" must be filed and proper notice given thereon prior to review. 8) Proposed order has not been filed.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/7/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of service has been filed. (3) Notice must also have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (4) Item 4c of the petition is incomplete. (5) Item 5a(2) of the petition is incomplete. (6) Item 12 of the petition is incomplete. (7) Petitioner does not address how property held in joint tenancy, which passes to the surviving joint tenant by operation of law, is community property. The couple's "financial and marital arrangements" without any substantiation is not sufficient legal authority. (Petitioner's separately filed declaration only repeats what is already in her petition and does not provide any legal authority or evidence.) (8) If the property is, in fact, community property, then only one-half should pass to the surviving spouse. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 4/23/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROBERT C. BERKE 1991 TRUST, AS AMENDED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee To Fill Vacancy
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although it would have been more compelling if the petitioner had a copy of a letter or some other proof of communication with the Bargmeyer firm or had given notice to that firm.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STEPHANIE CLEMONS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT JOHN GEARY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admission of the will. No appearance required. A status hearing is set for 4/22/2026. The personal representative shall file either a petition for an order for final distribution of the estate or a report of the status of administration in compliance with Prob. Code sec. 12200 et seq. two weeks before the hearing date.
